mappings.rationaleAI: The 1884-85 Berlin West Africa Conference directly established the rules for European partition of Africa (D.1), accelerating the imperialistic scramble for colonies and imposing arbitrary borders that disregarded ethnic and linguistic boundaries.

T.E. Lawrence's role as British liaison during the 1916 Arab Revolt against Ottoman rule connects to Ottoman dissolution in WWI (A.2.c), British encouragement of Arab nationalism and the postwar settlement (B.3.a), and the beginning of Zionist-Arab tensions as the postwar settlement reshaped Palestine (B.3.b).

The 1871 Mokrani Revolt was the largest anticolonial insurrection in French Algeria between conquest and independence, directly addressing French colonial suppression of the Muslim population, mass land confiscation, and accelerated European settler colonization in Algeria (B.2.a).

The broad overview of British imperial expansion explicitly addresses colonial governance structures, military conquest, and economic exploitation in Africa, providing essential context for the European scramble for African colonies (D.1), establishment of British colonies in West and East Africa (D.2, D.4), and the administrative systems that maintained imperial control.
